note: Sample work derived from the awesome-trading-agents list. These tasks exercise every router tier: cheap triage/classify, mid-tier extract/summarize, and strong analyze/synthesize/plan.

tasks.title: Bucket each project into Agents / MCPs / Skills
Flag which repos involve LIVE money vs paper/simulation
Tag market coverage (equities / crypto / prediction-markets / forex)
Extract the agent roles in the TradingAgents framework

payload.description: Multi-agent debate framework with analyst team, bull/bear researchers, trader, risk control, and portfolio manager roles debating before decisions.

tasks.title: Extract capabilities of the data-mcp knowledge harness

payload.description: Knowledge harness for AI-native finance with quant wiki, research papers, equity OHLCV, crypto data, and SEC filings.

tasks.title: Summarize FinRobot for a one-paragraph catalog blurb

payload.description: Open-source academic finance AI agent platform; multimodal analyst agents tied to FinGPT for stock research and forecasting.
Six-month forward-only experiment where ChatGPT managed a real US-equity micro-cap portfolio under strict rules.

tasks.title: Summarize the three MCP brokerage-execution servers
Compare single-agent vs multi-agent trading architectures

payload.question: When is the debate/portfolio-manager structure worth the extra cost vs a single self-improving agent?

tasks.title: Analyze risk controls across the agent projects

payload.question: What risk-management patterns recur, and where are the gaps for an autonomous trader?

tasks.title: Analyze the role of memory MCPs for trading agents

payload.description: Memory MCP recording decision rationale and outcomes for AI trading agents.
payload.question: How does persistent decision/outcome memory change agent performance and auditability?

tasks.title: Synthesize an architecture for an agent that uses these MCPs + Skills

payload.goal: Compose a research->backtest->paper-trade pipeline from the listed components.

tasks.title: Synthesize the key lessons from the Resources/papers section

payload.goal: What does the published evidence say about LLM trading-agent viability?

tasks.title: Plan a safe evaluation harness to vet these agents offline

payload.goal: Ordered plan to benchmark a candidate trading agent without risking capital.

tasks.title: Plan how to extend THIS agent factory to dispatch real trading-agent jobs

payload.goal: Turn seeded research tasks into a recurring pipeline that ingests the awesome-trading-agents list, refreshes it, and routes deep-dives to stronger models only when a project looks promising.
